title: "problems with mounting a NTFS partition"

forum: New to Ubuntu

### Post by LowSky on 2009-06-09
I used a Live CD to move around my partitions and to make my NTFS multimedia partition larger, now I am having problems with that drive auto-mounting at boot-up. No matter how many times I tell it to do so it will not.

here is my fstab

```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sdb5 :
UUID=ed3c125f-62e4-48d8-99f2-6ab4b33482ed / ext4 relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 1
LABEL=/home /home ext4 relatime 0 2
# Entry for /dev/ !! UNKNOW DEVICE !! :
UUID=bc336eae-b7d0-4c95-85b7-40f7f9530a61 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
/dev/sdb3 /media/sdb3 vfat users,user 0 0
/dev/sdb6 /media/sdb6 ext4 defaults 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /media/sdb1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
/dev/sdb7 /media/sdb7 swap defaults 0 0
```

here are screen-shots of gparted..

I have no idea why sda1 doesn't show up I assume its the sdb1 which I guess is the drive, and why sdb3 shows up as vfat, when its clearly a NTFS partition. Could I just relabel things to match what gparted says?

anyone know what I need to do?

some notes

/dev/sdb5 is /
/dev/sdb6 is /home
/dev/sbb7 is Swap
/dev/sdb3 is my Multimedia  partition
/dev/sda1 windows 7 partition

---

### Post by dioltas on 2009-06-09
If you moved the partitions around, then /dev/sdb1 might now be /dev/sdb3 etc.
I'd say chance relabelling them, just make sure to backup fstab so that if things get messed up you can restore it from a live cd.

---

### Post by LowSky on 2009-06-09
> **dioltas said:**
> If you moved the partitions around, then /dev/sdb1 might now be /dev/sdb3 etc.
I'd say chance relabelling them, just make sure to backup fstab so that if things get messed up you can restore it from a live cd.
yeah no... things are where they are and not relabeling themselves.

what I need is how to relabel fstab

would this work? if i reboot will my system hang or crash?
```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
/dev/sda1 /media/storage ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
/dev/sdb3 /media/multimedia ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
/dev/sdb5 / ext4 relatime,errors=remount-ro 0 1
/dev/sdb6 /home ext4 relatime 0 2
/dev/sdb7 /media/sdb7 swap defaults 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
```

---

### Post by LowSky on 2009-06-10
so I tried my idea I had above and it works getting the partition to automount on startup... anyhting else I might need to change or keep an eye on?
